response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Java) and a craftsmanship mindset, but the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust expertise for routing tile pipelines. The only C/C++ exposure is a 6-month stint at National Instruments back in 2014-2015, and there is no evidence of geospatial routing, navigation systems, or large-scale distributed data pipeline work in the required stack. The skills list does not include C++ or Rust at all.
strengths: Strong distributed/low-latency background at Dunnhumby handling millions of req/sec
Geospatial domain familiarity via GISCollective and Green Map work
Solid software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testability focus
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months in 2014-2015); no Rust
No demonstrated work on routing/navigation tile pipelines or map data transformation
Recent career trajectory is TypeScript/Node.js and AI tooling, not systems-level C++/Rust
